sprintGoal: Fix cross-project bleed in memclaw_recall phase 2. Currently CrossContextEnrich re-runs the query without caller_agent_id, so storage returns all scope_team/scope_org rows ranked by pure embedding similarity — a .53 login from an unrelated project can outrank the one you want. Fix: add preferred_agent_ids (list[str] | None) to memory_scored_search as a soft score boost (×1.2 for matching agent_id rows). CrossContextEnrich auto-populates it from caller_agent_id so same-project shared memories rank above cross-project noise without hard-filtering them out.

workItems.objective: Add preferred_agent_ids soft boost to memory_scored_search in postgres_service. Rows whose agent_id is in preferred_agent_ids get score multiplied by a configurable boost factor (default 1.2). Param is optional — None means no boost, preserving existing behaviour exactly.
acceptanceCriteria: memory_scored_search signature has preferred_agent_ids: list[str] | None = None and preferred_agent_boost: float = 1.2
When preferred_agent_ids is None, the SQL score expression is identical to before (no CASE added)
When preferred_agent_ids is set, a CASE expression multiplies score by preferred_agent_boost for rows where Memory.agent_id.in_(preferred_agent_ids), else 1.0
Boost is applied AFTER all existing multipliers (freshness, recall_boost, temporal_boost, status_penalty) so it does not interfere with their semantics
ruff check passes on the changed file

reasons: Additive param with None default — zero behaviour change when not set
Boost is a multiplier, not a filter — cross-project memories still appear, just ranked lower

payload.change: Add preferred_agent_ids: list[str] | None = None and preferred_agent_boost: float = 1.2 params. After building the score expression (line ~1281), wrap in a CASE: score * case((Memory.agent_id.in_(preferred_agent_ids), preferred_agent_boost), else_=1.0) when preferred_agent_ids is not None.

workItems.objective: Thread preferred_agent_ids and preferred_agent_boost from the /memories/scored-search HTTP request body through to the postgres_service call in the storage router.
acceptanceCriteria: scored_search endpoint in routers/memories.py passes preferred_agent_ids=body.get('preferred_agent_ids') and preferred_agent_boost=body.get('preferred_agent_boost', 1.2) to _svc.memory_scored_search()
No other scored-search call sites in the router are missed (check all usages of memory_scored_search in that file)
ruff check passes on the changed file

reasons: body.get() with defaults is safe — missing keys return None/default, existing callers unaffected
storage_client already POSTs the dict as-is, no client changes needed

payload.change: At the /scored-search endpoint (line ~130), add preferred_agent_ids=body.get('preferred_agent_ids') and preferred_agent_boost=body.get('preferred_agent_boost', 1.2) to the _svc.memory_scored_search() call. Check lines ~242 and ~318 for other memory_scored_search call sites in the same file and apply the same params.

workItems.objective: Update CrossContextEnrich to auto-populate preferred_agent_ids from caller_agent_id so phase 2 automatically biases toward the caller's own project without any MCP tool API change.
acceptanceCriteria: cross_context_enrich.py search_p2 dict includes preferred_agent_ids=[data['caller_agent_id']] when data.get('caller_agent_id') is not None, else the key is absent
Existing ponytail comment (line 56) is updated to note the preferred_agent_ids addition
When caller_agent_id is None (anonymous call), search_p2 has no preferred_agent_ids key — no boost applied
ruff check passes on the changed file

reasons: caller_agent_id is already in ctx.data (memory_service.py line 3164)
Adding one key to search_p2 dict is safe — storage ignores unknown keys via body.get()

payload.change: In search_p2 dict (line ~49-57): add 'preferred_agent_ids': [data['caller_agent_id']] if data.get('caller_agent_id') else key absent. Update the ponytail comment to: '# ponytail: no caller_agent_id → scope_team/scope_org only; preferred_agent_ids boosts same-project hits'

workItems.objective: Add tests proving (a) preferred_agent_ids boost ranks same-agent rows higher in postgres_service, and (b) CrossContextEnrich passes caller_agent_id as preferred_agent_ids in the phase 2 search payload.
acceptanceCriteria: test_preferred_agent_boost: given two scope_team rows with equal similarity, the one matching preferred_agent_ids scores higher after boost

test_cross_context_enrich_passes_preferred: mock sc.scored_search, run CrossContextEnrich with caller_agent_id set, assert search_p2 sent to scored_search contains preferred_agent_ids=[caller_agent_id]
test_cross_context_enrich_no_preferred_when_anon: caller_agent_id=None → preferred_agent_ids absent from search_p2
All three tests pass: pytest tests/test_recall_context.py -q
No existing tests broken: pytest tests/ -q (excluding pre-existing failures in test_integration.py)

reasons: Test-only changes, no production code affected

payload.note: postgres_service boost test can use direct SQL or mock the CASE expression. CrossContextEnrich tests mock get_storage_client() and inspect the call args.

workItems.objective: Deploy to 192.168.1.53 and verify phase 2 same-project bias works end-to-end: write two scope_team memories from different agent_ids with similar content, recall with cross_context=true, confirm the same-agent one ranks first.
acceptanceCriteria: docker compose build core-api + core-storage-api succeeds on 192.168.1.53
docker compose up -d brings both containers healthy
memclaw_recall with cross_context=true returns phase 2 hits where same-agent memories rank above cross-agent memories of comparable similarity
memclaw_recall with cross_context=false is unchanged (source_type=null for all, no preferred_agent_ids in play)

reasons: Restarts core-api and core-storage-api containers — brief downtime
core-storage-api restart affects all tenants on .53

payload.deploy_command: ssh 192.168.1.53 "cd ~/dev/caura-memclaw && git pull && docker compose build core-api core-storage-api && docker compose up -d core-api core-storage-api"

workItems.objective: Write SPRINT-RECALL-CONTEXT_SIGNOFF.md recording task outcomes, file changes, verification results, and backlog.

All RC-01..RC-05 outcomes recorded (state, commit, notes)
Verification section includes actual test run output
Backlog section lists any deferred items

warnings: RC-05 restarts core-storage-api which affects all tenants — schedule during low-traffic window
